description_long: This is a packaged loaf of bread encased in a rectangular, semi-transparent plastic bag with a blue and green color scheme. The packaging features a prominent label reading 'BETTER OFF BREAD' in white and orange, along with stylized yellow wheat graphics. The loaf inside is visibly sliced, with the individual slices discernible through the translucent side of the bag. The end of the bag is twisted and gathered, likely sealed to maintain freshness. The overall appearance suggests a standard commercial bread loaf, suitable for urban grocery stores or convenience shops. The packaging is smooth and glossy, typical of mass-produced bread products.
description: A sliced loaf of bread in a blue and green plastic bag labeled 'BETTER OFF BREAD', with visible wheat graphics and a twisted sealed end.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main label, wheat graphics, and the face of the loaf through the plastic.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the translucent plastic, revealing the sliced bread inside.
description_view_2: View of the twisted and gathered end of the plastic bag, showing the closure.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with the label and slices visible, and the sealed end extending outward.
attribute_crust_pattern: The crust is not directly visible due to the packaging, but the bread appears to have a uniform, light brown crust typical of mass-produced sandwich bread, with no visible seeds or flour dusting.
